tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58339,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document x-agent-session-id header and observability features added in Responses beta.2","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58335) by `@azure-sdk`, surfacing changes from PR #58314 by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58314 shipped with the beta.2 release several significant new features: - **`x-agent-session-id` response header** on all protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) per spec §8 - **Deterministic session ID derivation** — SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` → 63-char hex, with env var and random fallback - **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`** — logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers - **Startup configuration logging** — storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, and event stream TTL logged at startup - **Isolation key presence logging** — `HasUserIsolationKey` / `HasChatIsolationKey` flags in all endpoint logs (values never logged) - **Storage URL masking** — everything before `/storage` is redacted in diagnostic logs; only `api-version` query param retained - **Server version User-Agent** on outbound Foundry storage API requests via `ServerVersionRegistry` - **Inbound request logging** enabled automatically for Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups None of these features appear in the Responses package README, leaving developers without guidance on the new observability capabilities or the protocol-level `x-agent-session-id` header behavior. ## Gaps Found - **`x-agent-session-id` response header is undocumented** — The README describes `ResponseContext`, streaming modes, and the response lifecycle, but never mentions that every endpoint response includes `x-agent-session-id`. Developers building clients that depend on this header (per spec §8) have no README-level guidance. - **Deterministic session ID derivation not documented** — The algorithm (`"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` → SHA-256 → 63-char hex, with env var / random fallback) is defined in the CHANGELOG but absent from the README's Key concepts or Troubleshooting sections. - **Observability / Logging section missing** — The beta.2 release adds a full observability stack (startup logging, per-endpoint structured logging with isolation key presence flags, outbound storage logging with URL masking). The existing Troubleshooting section only mentions OpenTelemetry traces but does not describe the structured log output, what is masked, or how to interpret the new fields. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` **Action:** Edit — add three new subsections #### 1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` header subsection under "Key concepts" Insert after the "Thread safety" subsection (just before `## Examples`): ```markdown ### Session identity (`x-agent-session-id`) Every protocol endpoint response includes an `x-agent-session-id` response header. Its value is resolved in this order: 1. **Request payload** — `agent_session_id` field in the POST body (if provided) 2. **Environment variable** — `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` 3. **Deterministic hash** — SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters, where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` 4. **Random** — a random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available The header is always present on successful responses and on error responses (using the environment variable fallback for errors). Clients can use this header to correlate requests across conversation turns without needing to store the session ID explicitly. ``` #### 1b. Expand the "Logging" subsection under "Troubleshooting" Replace the existing two-sentence "Logging" paragraph with: ```markdown ### Logging **Inbound request logging** is enabled automatically for Tier 1 (`ResponsesServer.Run()`) and Tier 2 (`AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`) setups. Each request is logged with HTTP method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). Query strings are excluded to avoid logging sensitive data. **Endpoint structured logging** — all Responses API endpoints emit `Information`-level structured log entries that include the response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, and isolation key presence flags (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`). Key values are never logged. **Startup configuration logging** — when the host starts, the Responses library logs the configured storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, and event stream TTL at `Information` level. **Foundry storage request logging** — outbound calls to the Foundry storage API are logged via `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` with HTTP method, masked URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers. The URI is masked: everything before `/storage` is replaced with `***`, and all query parameters except `api-version` are stripped. This prevents leaking account, project, and token information in diagnostic output. **OpenTelemetry** — distributed traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source and exported when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for this documentation update. The changes are prose descriptions, not code samples. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` currently has these sections: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts (ResponseHandler, ResponseContext, ResponseEventStream, Streaming & Background Modes, Response Lifecycle, Thread safety) 3. ❌ **Missing:** Session identity / `x-agent-session-id` — add as new Key concepts subsection (Step 1a above) 4. ✅ Examples 5. ⚠️ **Incomplete:** Troubleshooting / Logging — expand to cover structured logging, startup logging, storage masking (Step 1b above) 6. ✅ Next steps 7. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24622380048/agentic_workflow) · ● 449K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58339","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-19T06:06:49Z","updated_at":"2026-04-19T06:06:49Z"}

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` package README following the beta.2 release, specifically missing documentation for the `x-agent-session-id` response header, deterministic session ID derivation algorithm, and the new observability/logging stack.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.2)
**Affected API:** README documentation — `x-agent-session-id` header, session ID derivation, `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, startup logging
**Scenarios:**
- Developers building clients that depend on the `x-agent-session-id` response header have no README-level guidance on its behavior
- The deterministic session ID derivation algorithm (SHA-256 of `{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}`) is in CHANGELOG but absent from README
- The beta.2 observability stack (startup logging, per-endpoint structured logging with isolation key presence flags, outbound Foundry storage logging with URL masking) is undocumented in the README

**Root ask:** Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` with three additions: a "Session identity" subsection under Key concepts, and an expanded "Logging" subsection under Troubleshooting.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue surfaced by an automated workflow (triggered by PR #58335 / PR #58314). No runtime bug or reproduction steps are needed.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58314 to confirm the full scope of changes shipped in beta.2
2. Apply the edits described in the Implementation Guide in the issue body to `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`
3. Run `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` to verify no build regressions
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` if any code snippets are added
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` is a data-plane client library (not `Azure.ResourceManager.*` or `Azure.Provisioning.*`)
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — the package lives under `sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS (line 85)
- **Confidence:** High — package name and service directory are explicitly stated in the issue; the CODEOWNERS `%AI Agents` entry unambiguously covers `sdk/agentserver/`
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — the `sdk/agentserver/` path and explicit `%AI Agents` label both confirm this match
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko, @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignment (ServiceOwners only); @mentioned both ServiceOwners via routing comment
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top from end of CODEOWNERS; all Mgmt-specific entries (lines ~912+) were skipped as the predicted category is "Client" not "Mgmt"; matched `%AI Agents` at line 85 before reaching any catch-all
</details>
